titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5968

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=760 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 21:10:50.607747+00 | translation moa=761 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 21:10:50.61726+00

div.p:
Omnibus Christi fidelibus ad quod presens scriptum peruenerint  Hugo leuedyman de lagehpe et
Eua vxor sua soror laurenci fili Willelmi filii Gocelini de Berewic salutem . Nouerit vniuersitas uestra
nos ad instanciam dicti laurenci concessisse . et hac presenti carta nostra Confirmasse . quietam
clamasse . et de nobis et heredibus nostris Inperpetuum omnino abiurasse Donacionem et Concessionem
Tocius terre sine aliquo Retinemento interritorio de Prendergest’ . quam laurencius filius
Willelmi filii Gocelini de Berewic habuit de dono Roberti fratris sui . Quam idem lauren
cius Dedit et Concessit domino Ricardo Priori et Conuentui de coldingham pro quadam suma pecu
nie sibi premanibus in magna necessitate sua pacata . Afidauimus eciam et Tactis sacrosanctis
Iurauimus pro nobis et heredibus nostris . quod nullo tempore seculi contra donacionem et Concessionem pred
ictam  venire presumemus . In Cuius Rei Testimonium  presenti scripto sigilla nostra appos
uimus . Hiis Testibus . Domino Willelmo de Mordingtona . Domino Dauid de Haddingtona . Domino Radulfo nob
ili . Domino Willelmo de scremerstona . Ada de Prendergest’ . Ada filio Iohannis de superyory eytona . Thoma de Nesbyt .
Bertramo de Ristona . Patricio scotto . Gilberto de lumesden’ . Iohanne filio Ingerami . Gregorio de Betyoc . et Aliis .

To all Christ's faithful to whom the present written document shall come, Hugh Levedyman of Langehope, and Eve his wife, sister of Laurence son of William son of Gocelin of Berwick, send greeting. Know us, all of you, at the urging of the said Laurence, to have granted, and by this our present charter to have made firm, quitclaimed, and for ourselves and our heirs entirely to have abjured for ever the donation and grant of the whole land without any withholding in the territory of Prenderguest which Laurence, son of William son of Gocelin of Berwick, had from the gift of Robert his brother, which the same Laurence gave and granted to the lord Richard the prior, and the convent of Coldingham, in return for a certain sum of money paid to him in advance on account of his great need. We have pledged and sworn, touching holy relics, for ourselves and our heirs, that we shall in no era of the world seek to move against the aforesaid donation and grant. As witness of this matter, we have applied our seals to the present document. As witnesses, the lord William of Mordington, the lord David of Haddington, the lord Ralph Noble, the lord William of Scremerston, Adam of Prenderguest, Adam son of John of Over Ayton, Thomas of Nisbet, Bertram of Reston, Patrick Scott, Gilbert of Lumsdaine, John son of Ingram, Gregory of Bethock, and others.
